div1.entryFree:
Fitly, 1) with propriety, reasonably, well: even so most f. as you malign our senators, Cor. I, 1, 116. cats, that can judge as f. of his worth as I can of those mysteries, IV, 2, 34. if aught within that little seeming substance, . . . and nothing more, may f. like your grace, Lr. I, 1, 203. I can compare our rich misers to nothing so f. as to a whale, Per. II, 1, 33.
2) pat to the purpose, opportunely: here, my lord. So f.? Tim. III, 4, 111. I will f. bring you to hear my lord speak, Lr. I, 2, 184.
